name: AWS WAF HTTP Method Enforcement Baseline
description: Baseline AWS WAF custom rule pack to enforce an allowlist of expected HTTP methods and block high-risk or unused verbs. Designed for staged rollout in COUNT mode before BLOCK enforcement.
objective: Reduce attack surface by only allowing expected HTTP methods per application surface and denying dangerous or unnecessary methods.
risk_mitigated: HTTP verb tampering and method confusion
Reconnaissance and proxy abuse via TRACE/TRACK/CONNECT
Unexpected method abuse against web routes and APIs

phases: Phase 1 (COUNT, 7-14 days): enable for all protected resources and log all non-allowlisted methods.
Phase 2 (TUNE): split by path class (web vs API), add required methods (e.g., OPTIONS for CORS, HEAD for health checks), verify integrations.
Phase 3 (BLOCK): switch high-confidence rules to BLOCK and retain temporary COUNT exceptions for known legacy paths.
Phase 4 (HARDEN): remove stale exceptions and periodically review method telemetry.

false_positive_tuning_notes: Web routes often only need GET/POST and occasionally HEAD; include HEAD if uptime probes or CDN checks use it.
APIs may require PUT/PATCH/DELETE and OPTIONS for CORS preflight. Scope broader allowlists to API path prefixes only (for example, /api/*).
If WebDAV or legacy integrations require methods such as PROPFIND or MKCOL, isolate to explicit paths and trusted source constraints.
Avoid global DELETE allowance unless business requirements are confirmed and monitored.
monitoring_guidance: Track top blocked/count methods by URI path and host.
Alert on spikes in TRACE/TRACK/CONNECT attempts.
Review OPTIONS and HEAD counts after CORS or load balancer changes.
Correlate blocked methods with 4xx/5xx API error increases after enforcement.

rules.statement: Block if method is TRACE, TRACK, or CONNECT.
For non-API paths, block when method is not in the web allowlist (GET, POST, HEAD).
rules.notes: Implement as path-scope NOT(OR(method matches allowlist)) with URI path exclusion for /api/* where needed.

rules.statement: For /api/* paths, block when method is not in API allowlist (GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE, OPTIONS, HEAD).
rules.notes: Tune by API stage and service ownership; remove DELETE/PATCH where not required.

terraform_example.description: Minimal AWS WAF v2 rule snippet aligned to repository conventions: start with COUNT, then move to BLOCK.
